name: vpn
title: Azure Hybrid Connectivity — VPN Gateway
summary: Creates site-to-site VPN connectivity in one of two modes:

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: resource_group_name
module.description: Name of the existing resource group for the VPN gateway.

module.name: location
module.description: Azure region for the VPN gateway.

module.name: attachment_type
module.description: Where the gateway lives: 'vnet' creates a classic virtual network gateway in gateway_subnet_id; 'vhub' creates a vWAN VPN gateway inside the virtual hub given by virtual_hub_id.

module.name: gateway_subnet_id
module.description: ID of the GatewaySubnet (must be named exactly 'GatewaySubnet', /27 or larger). Required when attachment_type = 'vnet'.

module.name: sku
module.description: VPN gateway SKU (VpnGw1, VpnGw2, VpnGw3, VpnGw1AZ, ...). vnet mode only; Basic is not supported by this module.

module.name: generation
module.description: Gateway generation: Generation1 or Generation2 (vnet mode; must match SKU compatibility).

module.name: active_active
module.description: Deploy active-active (two public IPs, two tunnels per connection side). vnet mode only; recommended for production.

module.name: virtual_hub_id
module.description: ID of an existing vWAN virtual hub (e.g., azure/transit/vwan hub_id output, supplied as a plain value). Required when attachment_type = 'vhub'.

module.name: virtual_wan_id
module.description: ID of the virtual WAN owning the hub. Required when attachment_type = 'vhub' (VPN sites are WAN-scoped).

module.name: vpn_gateway_scale_unit
module.description: Scale units of the vWAN VPN gateway (vhub mode). Each unit ~500 Mbps aggregate.

module.name: enable_bgp
module.description: Enable BGP on the gateway (vnet mode). In vhub mode BGP is per-connection (connections.enable_bgp); the hub router ASN is fixed at 65515.

module.name: bgp_asn
module.description: Azure-side BGP ASN (vnet mode; 65515 is the Azure default). vhub mode always uses the hub's 65515.

module.name: local_network_gateways
module.type: map(object({ gateway_address = string address_space = optional(list(string), []) bgp_asn = optional(number) bgp_peering_address = optional(string) }))
module.description: On-premises sites keyed by logical name. vnet mode renders these as local network gateways; vhub mode renders the SAME shape as VPN sites (gateway_address -> site link IP, bgp fields -> link BGP).

module.name: connections
module.type: map(object({ local_network_gateway_key = string shared_key = string enable_bgp = optional(bool, true) ipsec_policy = optional(object({ dh_group = string ike_encryption = string ike_integrity = string ipsec_encryption = string ipsec_integrity = string pfs_group = string })) }))
module.description: Site-to-site connections keyed by logical name, referencing sites by logical key. shared_key is sensitive. ipsec_policy applies in vnet mode only (vhub link policies are out of scope).

module.name: enable_management_lock
module.description: Apply a CanNotDelete management lock to the VPN gateway (governance/resource-locks).

outputs.name: gateway_id
outputs.description: ID of the gateway: virtual network gateway (vnet mode) or vWAN VPN gateway (vhub mode).

outputs.name: gateway_public_ips
outputs.description: Gateway public IPs (vnet mode; two when active_active). Empty in vhub mode — vWAN gateway instance IPs surface in the Azure portal/API after provisioning, not as a Terraform attribute.

outputs.name: local_gateway_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical site name to local network gateway ID (vnet mode). Empty in vhub mode.

outputs.name: vpn_site_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical site name to vWAN VPN site ID (vhub mode). Empty in vnet mode.

outputs.name: connection_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical connection name to connection ID (vnet or vhub mode).

outputs.name: bgp_peering_address
outputs.description: Azure-side BGP peering addresses of the gateway (vnet mode with BGP enabled), or null. In vhub mode the hub router (ASN 65515) owns the BGP sessions.
